test-run-tg-digest.note: Prior test run fully explored all packages. Current slices reuse validated boundaries.

slices.name: storage
slices.thesis: Map the persistence layer — SQLite storage, migrations, and all data access methods. This is the universal dependency hub with the highest fan-in in the codebase.
slices.seed_rationale: Core DB type and constructor; every other storage file depends on it
hints: storage.go is the fan-in hub — called by refresh, summarizer, source, tui, and main.go
channels.go is the second-highest fan-in file within storage
SQL migration files exist under migrations/ — these are .sql not .go
syncstate.go tracks refresh state for sources

slices.name: telegram-client
slices.thesis: Map the Telegram API client — authentication, session management, and message fetching. Wraps external Telegram library.
slices.seed_rationale: Main client struct and constructor; auth.go, messages.go, session.go extend it
hints: client.go has 8 inbound calls from main.go — heavily used during init
messages.go is called by source/hackernews, source/rss, source/reddit tests — likely shared message types
No outbound calls to other internal packages from production code except storage

slices.name: source-system
slices.thesis: Map the pluggable content source system — registry pattern, plus HackerNews, Reddit, RSS, and Telegram source implementations.
slices.seed_rationale: Registry pattern entry point; sources register here and are dispatched from here
hints: source.go likely defines the Source interface
Each subdirectory (hackernews, reddit, rss, telegram) is a concrete implementation
hackernews/hn.go and rss/rss.go both call storage.go and syncstate.go
source/telegram/telegram.go is distinct from internal/telegram — it's a source adapter

slices.name: summarizer
slices.thesis: Map the LLM summarization pipeline — service orchestration, prompt construction, and provider backends (Ollama, OpenRouter).
slices.seed_rationale: Orchestrates summarization with fan-out to 6 unique files including storage, providers, and source registry
hints: service.go is the orchestrator (6 unique call targets) — calls storage, channels, message_summaries, source/registry, openrouter
summarizer.go has no functions — likely interface/type definitions only
ollama.go and openrouter.go are LLM provider implementations
prompt.go handles prompt construction — likely template-based

slices.name: refresh-pipeline
slices.thesis: Map the data refresh pipeline — orchestrates fetching new content from sources and storing it.
slices.seed_rationale: Main refresh orchestrator with fan-out to 6 files across storage, source, and summarizer
hints: refresh.go calls storage.go (8), channels.go (5), syncstate.go (4) — heavy storage dependency
telegram.go within refresh is a helper, not the client — called by refresh.go (1 call)
This is a small but high-connectivity slice — 4 boundary packages for just 3 files

slices.name: tui
slices.thesis: Map the terminal UI — Bubble Tea app structure, views (dashboard, channels, help), filtering system, and keybindings.
slices.seed_rationale: App root with 9 unique call targets — secondary orchestrator after main.go
hints: app.go is a secondary orchestrator (9 fan-out) — calls storage, channels, filter, and cmd/digest
filter.go is a local hub within TUI — called by app.go, dashboard.go, filter_overlay.go
Bidirectional coupling: filter.go calls dashboard.go AND dashboard.go calls filter.go
channels.go ↔ dashboard.go also has bidirectional calls
Largest slice at 15 files — watch for internal complexity

slices.name: cli-orchestrator
slices.thesis: Map the CLI entry point and config — how the application boots, wires dependencies, and dispatches commands.
slices.seed_rationale: Application entry point with highest fan-out in codebase (19 unique call targets)
hints: main.go has 19 unique call targets — the god-file orchestrator
Contains helper functions: storeChannel, storeMessages, updateSyncState, ensureSourceChannel, buildLLMClient, RefreshAll, RefreshFiltered, SummarizeUnsummarized, SummarizeFiltered
config.go is only consumed by main.go — bundled here as the prior test run validated
tui/app.go calls back into cmd/digest/main.go (4 calls) — bidirectional coupling

execution_order.reason: Foundation — zero outbound internal deps, highest fan-in hub
Depends on storage, source, summarizer (all mapped)
Depends on storage + cmd/digest (bidirectional — run before cli-orchestrator so boundaries are known)
Depends on everything — run last when all boundaries are fully mapped
